message:
feat(acl): implement granular per-command and per-category ACL permissions

Replace the coarse full_access boolean with a fine-grained ACL permission
model supporting per-command (+cmd/-cmd) and per-category (+@cat/-@cat)
rules, matching Redis ACL semantics. Key changes:

- AclUser: replace `full_access` with `all_commands`, `all_keys`,
  `all_channels`, plus HashSets for allowed/denied commands and categories
- Add `is_command_allowed()` with proper precedence: explicit command
  deny > explicit command allow > category deny > category allow > base
- Add `commands_string()` for accurate ACL LIST/GETUSER serialization
- Add `command_categories()` helper in fr-runtime to resolve a command's
  ACL categories via COMMAND_TABLE flags and category listings
- ACL SETUSER: parse +@cat, -@cat, +cmd, -cmd, ~pattern, &pattern, reset
- ACL DRYRUN / GETUSER / LIST: reflect granular permissions accurately
- fr-command: expand @dangerous category with explicit command list
  (flushdb, flushall, keys, shutdown, debug, sort, etc.) and add
  flushall to @server category
- Apply rustfmt formatting across all 8 touched crates

Adds 10 new tests covering per-command allow/deny, category-based
permissions, category deny overriding +@all, per-command overriding
category deny, GETUSER/LIST reflection, case-insensitive matching,
allcommands reset, and ACL reset rule.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
